rationale: The cited paper is used in the core theory-building sections, not just in a background list: the citing paper relies on it to define and motivate the monitoring capability construct and to support the argument that some monitoring approaches can trigger reactance. It is also used in the methods section as a source for operationalization (adapted opportunism/monitoring-related measures). These roles indicate meaningful conceptual and methodological dependence rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper builds its monitoring-related logic partly on Heide, Wathne, and Rokkan (2007), using that work to frame how monitoring affects channel behavior and to justify moderation arguments in the punishment model. It also borrows measurement foundations from the cited study.
